customer: Commonwealth of Australia as represented by the Department of Defence
executive_summary:
Fujitsu acknowledges the critical need for alignment with the Commonwealth's governance standards, particularly within the Department of Defence's stringent framework. Our solution is built upon a robust governance framework that ensures compliance with ISO/IEC 27002:2022, safeguarding sensitive Defence information through comprehensive security controls. These controls, including role-based access control, data encryption, and user authentication, are integrated across all delivery phases, ensuring security is a foundational component.

To address governance requirements, Fujitsu will implement a structured methodology featuring regular performance reviews and progress meetings. These sessions will be essential for assessing adherence to change processes and compliance, as detailed in the Contract Reports. Our Supplier Contract Manager will facilitate these processes, ensuring timely preparation and delivery of agendas and draft minutes, while incorporating feedback from the Commonwealth to maintain transparency and accountability.

Fujitsu's extensive experience in delivering ICT services within Defence environments positions us as a trusted partner. We are equipped to conduct thorough post-implementation reviews, evaluating the effectiveness and efficiency of changes to ensure continuous improvement and alignment with Defence objectives. Our national workforce, comprising over 1,100 security-cleared personnel, is strategically positioned to support complex Defence environments across Australia. This capability, combined with our long-standing partnership with Defence, enables us to deliver integrated solutions that meet the unique demands of both enterprise and deployed operational contexts.
main_response_section:
Fujitsu recognises the critical importance of aligning with the Commonwealth's governance standards, particularly in the context of the Department of Defence's stringent requirements. Our approach is underpinned by a robust governance framework that ensures compliance with ISO/IEC 27002:2022, thereby safeguarding sensitive Defence information through comprehensive security controls embedded across all phases of delivery. This includes role-based access control, data encryption, and user authentication, ensuring that security is a foundational component rather than an afterthought.

In addressing the requirement for governance, Fujitsu will implement a structured methodology that includes regular performance reviews and progress meetings. These sessions will be pivotal in assessing adherence to change processes and compliance, as outlined in the Contract Reports. Our Supplier Contract Manager will ensure timely preparation and delivery of agendas and draft minutes, incorporating feedback from the Commonwealth to maintain transparency and accountability.

Furthermore, Fujitsu's extensive experience in delivering ICT services across Defence environments positions us as a trusted partner capable of conducting thorough post-implementation reviews. These reviews will evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of changes, ensuring continuous improvement and alignment with Defence objectives.

Our national workforce, comprising over 1,100 security-cleared personnel, is strategically positioned to support complex Defence environments across Australia. This capability, combined with our long-standing partnership with Defence, enables us to deliver integrated solutions that meet the unique demands of both enterprise and deployed operational contexts.
